Package org.unlaxer.tramli

  • Method Details

    • initiallyAvailable

      public FlowDefinition.Builder<S> initiallyAvailable(Class<?>... types)
      Declare data types that will be provided via startFlow(initialData).

    • externallyProvided

      public FlowDefinition.Builder<S> externallyProvided(Class<?>... types)
      Declare data types injected via resumeAndExecute(externalData), not available at start.

    • ttl

      public FlowDefinition.Builder<S> ttl(Duration ttl)

    • maxGuardRetries

      public FlowDefinition.Builder<S> maxGuardRetries(int max)

    • from

      public FlowDefinition.Builder<S>.FromBuilder from(S state)

    • onError

      public FlowDefinition.Builder<S> onError(S from, S to)

    • onStepError

      public FlowDefinition.Builder<S> onStepError(S from, Class<? extends Exception> exceptionType, S to)
      Route specific exception types to specific states. Checked before onError.

    • onStateEnter

      public FlowDefinition.Builder<S> onStateEnter(S state, Consumer<FlowContext> action)
      Callback when entering a state (pure data/metrics, no I/O).

    • onStateExit

      public FlowDefinition.Builder<S> onStateExit(S state, Consumer<FlowContext> action)
      Callback when exiting a state (pure data/metrics, no I/O).

    • onAnyError

      public FlowDefinition.Builder<S> onAnyError(S errorState)

    • allowPerpetual

      public FlowDefinition.Builder<S> allowPerpetual()
      Allow perpetual flows (no terminal states). Skips path-to-terminal validation.

    • allowUnreachable

      public FlowDefinition.Builder<S> allowUnreachable()
      Allow unreachable states (shared enum across multiple flows). Skips reachability check.

    • strictMode

      public FlowDefinition.Builder<S> strictMode()
      Declare that this flow should run in strict mode (produces verification).

    • build

      public FlowDefinition<S> build()

    • buildAndValidate

      public FlowDefinition.BuildResult<S> buildAndValidate()
      Build without throwing. Returns definition (if valid) + structured errors.
